message:
fix(lab/snapshot): hash full serialized content for integrity verification

The snapshot integrity hash previously only incorporated the timestamp and
entity counts (task_count, resource_count, timer_count). This meant an
attacker or bug could alter task states, resource values, or timer configs
while preserving the same entity counts, and the integrity check would
not detect the tampering.

Now, compute_integrity_hash() serializes the entire snapshot to JSON via
serde_json::to_vec() and feeds every byte through the FNV-1a hash. This
detects any semantic change to the snapshot content — not just structural
changes like adding/removing entities. A fallback path preserves the old
count-based hashing if serialization fails (should not happen in practice).

Test: integrity_verification_detects_semantic_tampering creates a snapshot,
records its hash, then mutates a task state from Running to Completed
(same entity count), and verifies the hash changes, proving semantic
tampering is now detected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
